description: Head-receipt verification MUST fail when receipt.registry_did does not bind to the authority the response was fetched from AND to capabilities.registry_did (RFC-ACDP-0011 §7 step 3). A head receipt is only evidence about the registry that served it; accepting one naming a different authority would let a hostile registry replay another registry's (perfectly signed) head attestations as its own. Same authority-binding principle as rcpt-004 and fed-006.

input.scenario: Consumer calls GET https://hostile.example/lineages/lin:sha256:c7fef01c…/current and the response carries a lineage_head_receipt whose registry_did names registry.example.com (the lhr-001 golden receipt, replayed verbatim).

expected.behavior: §7 step 3: registry_did MUST be did:web:<authority> where <authority> equals the authority the response was fetched from and equals capabilities.registry_did. did:web:registry.example.com ≠ hostile.example → the head receipt MUST be reported as failed regardless of whether its signature verifies under registry.example.com's key.

additional_failure_cases.case: registry_did uses a method other than did:web (e.g. did:key)
additional_failure_cases.expected_outcome: failure — registry_did is did:web-only (RFC-ACDP-0011 §4, following RFC-ACDP-0010 §4).
failure — the receipt key must belong to the attesting registry's DID document (§7 steps 2–3).
failure — lineages are single-registry; a registry cannot attest heads for another authority's contexts (§7 step 3).

expected.verdict_independence: Body and RFC-ACDP-0010 receipt verdicts independent, as always (RFC-ACDP-0011 §7).
